title: Buildah in Tekton task is failing with: unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Invalid argument
body:
buildah version: multiple (1.16, 1.21, 1.22)

Tekton Pipelines version 1.22 (OpenShift Pipelines 1.4.1)

We have observed three OpenShift clusters where buildah starts failing with the following errors:

```bash
unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Invalid argument
ERRO error parsing PID "": strconv.Atoi: parsing "": invalid syntax
ERRO (unable to determine exit status)
```

Once it starts failing, it always fails.  We don't believe that the issue is directly related to buildah, but hope that someone might have a clue.  It's almost like underlying permissions are being affected for the pipeline service account running with the pipeline-scc SecurityContextConstraint.

The following Tekton `Task` works on the other clusters.  It fails with the above error in the clusters where the issue has manifested itself.

```yaml
apiVersion: tekton.dev/v1beta1
kind: Task
metadata:
  name: buildah-test
spec:
  stepTemplate:
    volumeMounts:
    - name: varlibc
      mountPath: /var/lib/containers
  steps:
  - name: prep-workingdir
    image: quay.io/buildah/stable:latest
    imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    script: |
      chmod 777 /workspace/source
      cp -r /tekton/creds/.docker /workspace/source
      chown -R 1000:1000 /workspace/source/.docker
    env:
    - name: user.home
      value: /workspace/source
    workingDir: "/workspace/source"
  - name: build-image
    image: quay.io/buildah/stable:latest
    securityContext:
      runAsUser: 1000
    imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    script: |
      export HOME=/workspace/source
      export BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot
      APP_NAME=test
      DESTINATION_IMAGE="image-registry.openshift-image-registry.svc:5000/$(context.taskRun.namespace)/${APP_NAME}:latest"
      buildah --version
      BUILDAH_ARGS="--storage-driver vfs"
      CONTAINER=$(buildah ${BUILDAH_ARGS} from quay.io/buildah/stable:latest )
      # CONTAINER=$(buildah --storage-driver vfs --log-level trace --userns-uid-map $(id -u):$(( $(id -u) + 1 )):65536 --userns-gid-map $(id -u):$(( $(id -u) + 1 )):65536 --tls-verify=false from quay.io/buildah/stable:latest )
      cat << EOF > ./test.sh
      #!/bin/bash
      echo "hello"
      EOF
      chmod 750 ./test.sh
      buildah ${BUILDAH_ARGS} copy ${CONTAINER} ./test.sh /application.sh
      buildah ${BUILDAH_ARGS} config --entrypoint '["/application.sh"]' --port 8080 ${CONTAINER}
      buildah ${BUILDAH_ARGS} config --label GIT_COMMIT="Hello" --author="Tekton" ${CONTAINER}
      buildah ${BUILDAH_ARGS} commit ${CONTAINER} ${DESTINATION_IMAGE}
      buildah ${BUILDAH_ARGS} unmount ${CONTAINER}
      buildah ${BUILDAH_ARGS} push ${DESTINATION_IMAGE} docker://${DESTINATION_IMAGE}
    env:
    - name: user.home
      value: /workspace/source
    workingDir: "/workspace/source"
  volumes:
  - name: varlibc
    emptyDir: {}
```

I posted this on the Tekton Operator project as well.

https://github.com/tektoncd/operator/issues/381

comments.body:
Could you check to see if there are seccomp or selinux avc errors in the audit.log when this fails.

ausearch -m seccomp
ausearch -m avc

Could this be an older kernel?

@giuseppe WDYT?

We pinned a pod to a specific node and monitored both `journalctl` and `/var/log/audit/audit.log`

We didn't see anything.  But in the morning we'll look specifically for seccomp and avc.

This base OS is RHCOS on OCP 4.7.9 and ROSA 4.7.19

No output from either of:

```bash
ausearch -m seccomp
ausearch -m avc
```

Kernel info: `Linux ip-10-112-120-43 4.18.0-240.22.1.el8_3.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Mar 25 14:36:04 EDT 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux`

Additional trace level output:

```bash
running [buildah-in-a-user-namespace --log-level trace from scratch]

container=oci STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs _BUILDAH_STARTED_IN_USERNS= BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot HOME=/root TMPDIR=/var/tmp _CONTAINERS_USERNS_CONFIGURED=1], UID map [{ContainerID:0 HostID:0 Size:4294967295}], and GID map [{ContainerID:0 HostID:0 Size:4294967295}]"
```

Here is a much simpler test to reproduce.

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: buildah-bare-test
spec:
  serviceAccountName: pipeline
  restartPolicy: Never
  containers:
    - name: buildah
      image: registry.redhat.io/rhel8/buildah@sha256:6a68ece207bc5fd8db2dd5cc2d0b53136236fb5178eb5b71eebe5d07a3c33d13
      command: ["buildah", "from", "scratch"]
      volumeMounts:
      - mountPath: /var/lib/containers
        name: varlibcontainers
  volumes:
  - emptyDir: {}
    name: varlibcontainers
```

Same error output:

```bash
Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): Invalid argument
level=error msg="error parsing PID \"\": strconv.Atoi: parsing \"\": invalid syntax"
level=error msg="(unable to determine exit status)"
```

Additional Info:

Kernel info is the same on working and non-working clusters:

`Linux ip-10-112-46-126 4.18.0-240.22.1.el8_3.x86_64 #1 SMP Thu Mar 25 14:36:04 EDT 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux`

The non-working clusters were working...  so, something changed, but we have no idea what it could be.

Additional info:

These clusters are running in AWS.  We are seeing that in the "broken" clusters, vanilla S2I builds are failing too with file permission issues in the init-container...  Starting to think that this is not isolated to buildah.

We're running another test overnight to see if maybe this is actually related to something in the AWS account.

I'll update this issue with the results.

We have not yet found a definitive root cause.  However, the S2I build failures were caused by a misconfigured SCC that was created as part of a different operator installation.

So, at this point we are confident that the issue was self-inflicted.

Recreating the OpenShift Cluster resolved the problem.

If we find a definitive root cause, I'll update this issue so that others can benefit.
